title: Multiple Dump Tank Jet Siphon Operation

hoses: Water tender dumping into left portable dump tank
Jet siphon mounted at the left portable dump tank
Black hard suction transfer hose connecting the left dump tank to the right dump tank through the jet siphon setup
Small supply hose from Engine 181 discharge to the jet siphon at the left dump tank
Large black hard suction hose from the right dump tank to Engine 181 intake
Attack line: 200 ft of 1.75-inch hose from Engine 181 discharge to firefighter operating near the rural structure fire
appliances: Jet siphon mounted at left dump tank
Water tender actively dumping into left dump tank
Second water tender approaching on rural driveway
sceneElements.elevation: Level rural driveway operation, 0 PSI elevation correction

problems.title: Attack Line Pump Pressure
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying one 200 ft 1.75-inch attack line with a ChiefXD 185 fog nozzle operating at 185 GPM @ 50 PSI. What pump discharge pressure should be set?
formulaBreakdown: PP = FL + NP + elevation + appliance loss.
FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds.
problems.instructorExplanation: The attack line calculation is based on the hose between Engine 181 and the nozzle. The dump tanks, jet siphon, and hard suction are part of the water supply side and are not added to the attack line pump pressure.
problems.explainMistake: Do not include the dump tanks, jet siphon, or hard suction in the pump pressure calculation for the attack line. Use attack-line friction loss plus nozzle pressure.

problems.title: Usable Tender Water
problems.question: Each water tender carries 3000 gallons. Using the 90% usable water rule, how many gallons are usable from one tender?
problems.instructorExplanation: For tender shuttle calculations, FirePumpSim counts 90% of the tender capacity as usable water. A 3000-gallon tender provides 2700 usable gallons.
problems.explainMistake: Do not use the full tender capacity. Use the 90% usable water rule for shuttle calculations.

problems.title: Single Tender Shuttle Flow
problems.question: One tender provides 2700 usable gallons and has a 12-minute shuttle cycle time. What sustained flow can one tender provide?
formulaBreakdown: Tender shuttle GPM = usable gallons / cycle time.
problems.instructorExplanation: Tender shuttle flow is based on usable gallons divided by total cycle time. One tender in this setup can sustain 225 GPM.
problems.explainMistake: Do not divide the full tank capacity by cycle time. Use the 2700 usable gallons value.

problems.title: Tenders Needed
problems.question: The operation needs a sustained water supply of 450 GPM. If each tender can sustain 225 GPM, how many tenders are needed?
formulaBreakdown: Tenders needed = required sustained flow / flow per tender.
problems.instructorExplanation: Two tenders are needed because each tender can sustain 225 GPM and the operation requires 450 GPM.
problems.explainMistake: Do not count only the tender currently dumping. Shuttle operations require enough tenders cycling to maintain the required sustained flow.

problems.title: Combined Dump Tank Water Duration
problems.question: The two portable dump tanks are connected by a jet siphon and function as one water source. If each dump tank contains 2500 gallons and Engine 181 is flowing 185 GPM, how long will the combined dump tank supply last with no refill?
problems.instructorExplanation: Because the dump tanks are connected with a jet siphon, they function as one water source. With 5000 total gallons and a 185 GPM flow, the supply lasts about 27 minutes with no refill.
problems.explainMistake: Do not calculate using only one dump tank when the jet siphon connects both tanks into a single water source.

problems.title: Drafting Tank Identification
problems.question: Engine 181 is drafting through the large black hard suction from only one dump tank. How many dump tanks are directly connected to Engine 181 by hard suction?
formulaBreakdown: The large black hard suction connects Engine 181 to the right dump tank only.
The left dump tank transfers water through the jet siphon setup.
Direct drafting tanks connected to Engine 181 = 1.
problems.instructorExplanation: Engine 181 drafts from only one dump tank. The second dump tank supports the drafting tank through the jet siphon, allowing both tanks to function together without two hard suction intakes.
problems.explainMistake: Do not count both dump tanks as directly drafted. Only the tank connected to Engine 181 by the large hard suction is directly drafted.
